content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 9, 2017

Denise Athas
President and Broker
Atlas & Associates, Inc.
1202 Grant Avenue, A2
Novato, CA 94945

dathas@novato.org

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-17-092

Dear Ms. Athas:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090. Additionally, we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate.

QUESTION

Do the conflict of interest provisions under the Act prohibit you from participating in the Northwest Quad Area zoning amendment proposal before the City of Novato?

CONCLUSION

No, under the Act you do not have a conflict of interest in the decision based on your financial interests and you may participate.

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-17-092
Page No. 2

FACTS

You are the Mayor and a City councilmember for the City of Novato. You are a real estate broker and your spouse is a real estate agent. Together you own a real estate brokerage firm, Athas & Associates Real Estate Inc. Your business employs yourself, your spouse, and four commission-basis licensed real estate associates. Your business, Athas & Associates, leases an office building on 1202 Grant Avenue in Novato. The current lease expires in September, 2021, with a base monthly rent of $1,748. The lease entitles you to one parking space on the premises, designated with your name. Athas & Associates is permitted to sublet the building with the consent of the landlord and for an amount not to exceed your base rent per month.

This office building is located immediately adjacent to the Northwest Quad Area, an area for which the City staff is presenting a revised zoning proposal to the City Council. The City Staff Report, "Draft Form-Based Zoning Code for Northwest Quadrant Neighborhood," dated March 15, 2017 provides the following background on the area: The Northwest Quad Area was developed in the first half of the 20th Century with small single-family bungalows. But in the 1960's and '70's, a substantial portion of the neighborhood was rapidly developed with "relatively inexpensive and poorly-designed" apartment buildings. (Staff Report, p. 1.) To slow the development, the City enacted reforms, such as prohibiting the removal of any "sound" single-family residence for redevelopment, and the area's current zoning (Medium Density Multi Family (R10-4.5)), which allows up to 10 units per acre. (Id., p. 4.) Because most of the existing apartment sites exceed this allowed density, currently the apartments cannot be replaced without reducing the number of units. (Id., p.12.) Because of these land use limitations, the City states, "there has been virtually no new development in the past 35 years" despite its "ideal location" near downtown. (Id., p. 3, and Attachment 3, Neighborhood Study, p.1.)

The proposed rezoning of the area is in response to public support to "spur investments in the neighborhood" while redeveloping on a compatible scale to the existing single-family homes. (Staff Report, p. 2.) The City proposes to adopt "form-based" zoning regulations² and design guidelines to allow compatible development within the Medium Density Multi-Family density range of 10 to 20 units per acre. These proposed zoning amendments would be more prescriptive in terms of detailing the specifics of the mass and placement of buildings allowed to be constructed or redeveloped in the Northwest Quad Area to increase predictability in the design review process. (Id., p. 13.) The proposed rezoning would also eliminate the single-family home demolition restriction.

You stated in a subsequent telephone conversation that while these proposals are intended to "spur redevelopment," the City's proposals will primarily give current owners, and potential developers parameters for redevelopment that have been lacking in the past. You state that this will be a long-term revitalization project for the area, and that there are still factors which will make

² A "form-based code," also known as a "traditional neighborhood development code," is defined as a land development regulation that fosters predictable built results and a high-quality public realm by using physical form (rather than separation of uses) as the organizing principle for the code. By comparison, traditional (called "Euclidian") zoning codes are focused more on defining and restricting allowable land uses in an effort to prevent conflicts between adjacent uses of properties (e.g., industrial next to residential) and regulate the sizes of potential structures by defining a three dimensional "box" formed by minimum building setbacks from property lines, a height limit, as well as the percent of building coverage of a parcel. (Staff Report, supra, p. 2.)

File No. A -17-092
Page No. 3

redevelopment or in-fill development cost prohibitive. There are only a few vacant lots in the area.
Fire codes require turn-arounds that many of the lots cannot accommodate given the lots' long and
unusual sizes. Many of these long lots contain homes and multi-unit complexes that need extensive
repairs. You state that, "it will be difficult to make the numbers work" in terms of redevelopment.
As a result, you do not anticipate the zoning changes will bring an increase in business for your real
estate firm. You note that there are seven realty businesses near you, and that your business serves
multiple counties.

You assert that the adoption of the proposed amendments to the Northwest Quad Area
zoning will not do any of the following: (1) change the termination date of Athas & Associates'
lease; (2) increase or decrease the potential rental value of the leased property; (3) increase or
decrease the rental value of the rental property; (4) change Athas & Associates' or your use of the
real property subject to the lease; or (5) impact your use and enjoyment of the leased property or the
exclusive parking space assigned to you.

You also state that within the last 12 months neither Athas & Associates nor any of its
associates had any listings for the sale of real property located in the Northwest Quad Area. Neither
you nor any of the associates working for Athas & Associates acted as a participating broker in any
transaction for the sale or lease of real property located in the Northwest Quad Area. Further,
neither you, nor Athas & Associates received any income from any transaction involving real
property located in the Northwest Quad Area during the last 12 months. You were involved in only
one transaction in the area, several years ago, and do not have a special expertise or business
relationship to the area.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest.
(Section 87103.) A conflict of interest may arise only when a reasonably foreseeable financial effect
of a governmental decision on a public official's interests is material, and distinguishable from the
effect on the public generally.

Section 87103 defines financial interests to include:

• An interest in a business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment of
$2,000 or more (Section 87103(a)); or in which the official is a director, officer, partner,
trustee, employee, or holds any position of management (Section 87103(d)).

• An interest in real property in which the official has a direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or
more (Section 87103(b)), including a pro rata share of interests in real property of any
business entity or trust in which the individual or immediate family owns, directly,
indirectly, or beneficially, a 10-percent interest or greater (Section 82033).

• An interest in a source of income to the official, or promised income, which aggregates to
$500 or more within 12 months prior to the decision (Section 87103(c)) including any
community property interest in the income of a spouse and a pro rata share of the income of

File No. A -17-092
Page No. 4

any business entity or trust in which the official (or his or her spouse) owns directly a 10
percent interest or greater. (Section 82030(a).)

• An interest in the official's personal finances, including those of the official's immediate
family. This is known as the "personal financial effects" rule. (Section 87103.)

You have a financial interest in Athas & Associates, your business entity and a source of
income; and in your real property leasehold interest. Your facts indicate that neither you, your
associates, agents, nor your business, has received commission income related to a real estate
transaction in the Northwest Quad Area in the past twelve months. ³

To determine if you have a prohibited conflict of interest under the Act we determine
whether it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on your
business and real property interests. (Regulation 18700(d).)

Foreseeability and Materiality

The standard for foreseeability differs depending on whether an interest is "explicitly" or
"not explicitly" involved in a decision. (Regulation 18701.) Here, your financial interests are not
explicitly involved in the decision, and the appropriate standard to determine foreseeability is
"whether the financial effect can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than hypothetical
or theoretical." (Regulation 18701(b).) The standard for materiality is particular to the type of
financial interest involved. (Regulation 18702.)

Real property:

Regulation 18702.2(b) provides that a decision's effect on a leasehold financial interest is
material if the decision will:

(1) Change the termination date of the lease;

(2) Increase or decrease the potential rental value of the property;

(3) Increase or decrease the rental value of the property, and the official has a
right to sublease the property;

(4) Change the official's actual or legally allowable use of the real property;

(5) Impact the official's use and enjoyment of the real property.

None of the facts provided indicate that the Northwest Quad Area re-zoning decision will
alter the period, or value of the lease. Although Athas & Associates has the right to sublease the

³ Please note that if you receive commission income as a broker, agent, or salesperson for a specific sale or
similar transaction you may have interests in both the person the agent represents, and the business entity under whose
auspices he or she works. (Regulation 18700.1 (c).)

File No. A -17-092
Page No. 5

property, the terms of the lease limit the sublease rent to the base rent of the lease. Nor does it
appear that the use or enjoyment of the property will be affected.

Business Entity:

Regulation 18702.1(b) provides that a decision's effect on an official's business interest is
material "if a prudent person with sufficient information would find it reasonably foreseeable that
the decision's effect would contribute to a change in the value of the privately-held business entity."
That regulation also provides examples of the types of decisions that may impact the analysis, such
as decisions that would "increase or decrease the need for the products or services that the business
entity supplies." (Regulation 18702.1(b)(3).)

Athas & Associates is a real estate business in the City, and located adjacent to the
Northwest Quad Area. However, your business is one of seven realty businesses in the immediate
area. It has only been involved in one transaction involving a Northwest Quad parcel in years past
and none in the past 12 months. Moreover, while the re-zoning decision is intended to act as a spur
to re-development of the area, your facts indicate that the decision will not necessarily translate into
increased business for your real estate business. The decision will remove some impediments to
redevelopment, and allow owners and investors more certain parameters for redevelopment. But
with the cost-prohibitive factors you have described of older, dilapidated structures and odd lots, the
redevelopment is anticipated to be complicated, and on a incremental basis. Based on these facts,
a prudent person with this information is not likely to find that the re-zoning proposal for the
Northwest Quad Area would contribute to a change in the value of your real estate business, Athas
& Associates.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

By: L. Karen Harrison
Counsel, Legal Division

LKH:jgl
